rationale: The citing paper includes retirement saving as a key predictive-validity outcome (it tests and reports that lay rationalism predicts higher retirement-account saving), while the cited paper is a well-known experimental study directly about increasing retirement saving via future-self renderings. Given this direct construct/domain overlap (saving behavior and intertemporal financial choice), the citation could plausibly serve as a substantive theory/mechanism benchmark rather than a purely incidental mention.
